_about: Fresh Claude-authored gold labels for a stratified Memex sample. Labels reflect judgement from note CONTENT (frontmatter is stripped before the model sees a note and is not trusted as ground truth). `folder` is the top-level Memex folder the note should live in (used for assign-folder). Expand this set toward ~45 notes by adding entries in the same shape. Optional keys: person_kind (person notes), summary (reference for cosine), refine (include in refine-body), hub (enables assign-hub), people (extraction gold).

notes.summary: A structured outline for a Buddhist meditation session, walking through refuge in the Three Jewels, the Four Noble Truths, and somatic awareness cues.
Dated therapy journal entry reflecting on family conflict, an absent advisor, and a self-critical habit of dwelling on how finished work could have been done better.

notes.summary: A 2013 journal entry describing recurring half-awake sleep disturbances in which the author tries to solve problems or perform tasks like making caramel brittle.
Jon Peterson's 2012 book Playing at the World, a detailed history of wargaming and role-playing games.
A 2021 journal article by Priteegandha Naik analysing Priya Sarukkai Chabria's novel Generation 14 through Dalit feminism and Indian science fiction.
A reusable LLM deep-research prompt for finding practical how-to resources on community-based participatory and community-engaged energy research.
Lindsay Baker, VP and Head of Sustainability & Wellbeing at WeWork, an industry veteran in healthy and sustainable buildings.
Edward Mazria, architect, author, and researcher who founded Architecture 2030 and is known for seminal work on the built environment's energy use and emissions.
The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), the United Nations body for assessing climate science.

notes.summary: Guidelines for durable personal data storage and retrieval, favouring plain-text and open standards, normalization, canonical forms, and round-trippability.
A personal checklist of wellbeing and ethical-living commitments across body, environment, and charitable giving.
An explainer of the Four Noble Truths and the Noble Eightfold Path in Buddhism.
